comment_text.sn3.21:2.2: Caṇḍālas (“corpse-workers”) are frequently depicted as the lowest of untouchables as seen by Brahmins. However, the usual rendering of “outcast” is unsatisfactory, as all the groups here are defined positively by their occupation, and moreover some, such as the scavengers, would also have been untouchable. The Pali commentaries equate the caṇḍāla with the chavaka (or chapaka) of Bu Sk 69:1.12.1, a “corpse disposer” (chavachaḍḍakacaṇḍālā; see also chavakacaṇḍālo at Mil 5.4.7:2.4). They were known for their “bone-washing” ceremony (DN 1:1.13.2, AN 10.107:1.1). This agrees with Rāmāyana 1.58.10, which describes a caṇḍāla as dark-complexioned, dirty, with disheveled hair, his body smeared with graveyard ash. Mahāvaṁsa 10.91–94 tells how King Paṇḍukābhaya employed caṇḍālas for cleaning streets and toilets, bearing the dead, and watching over cemeteries, with their village adjoining the cemetery. My grandfather was a corpse-worker, so in another world it might have been my inheritance.
comment_text.sn3.21:3.1: Note the rare term lohitamala, “bloodstain”.
comment_text.sn3.21:7.1: The last phrase is curious. It doesn’t mirror the positive version of the simile, and it’s not clear exactly what it means. Of course it can be interpreted spiritually, but it’s a simile, the point is that it is describing something physical. Bodhi interprets it as “underground darkness”, but he misses the change in verb from “descend” to “enter”; and anyway, the text doesn’t say “underground”. Commentary is silent.
comment_text.sn3.21:15.5: The tidiva was the realm of Indra/Sakka (DN 21:1.12.22, DN 30:2.27.12, Thag 10.1:8.4), as it was from the time of the Vedas (Rig Veda 9.113.9, Atharva Veda 10.9.5, 17.1.10). In Buddhist cosmology, this agrees with the fact that Sakka’s realm is the third of the heavens (after the earth gods and the four great kings). Thus the Third Heaven is the heaven of the Thirty-Three. Both ideas spring from an old association of Indra, or divinity generally, with the number three. Agni Purāṇa lists tridiva as a synonym for “heaven”, as does Amarakośa 1.1.11. Kṣīrasvāmin clarifies that three here means “third”, while also adding that some connect it with the Brahmā realm (Amarakośodghāṭana 1.1.6; see SN 7.18:4.2).

root_text.sn3.21:1.2: Atha kho rājā pasenadi kosalo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho rājānaṁ pasenadiṁ kosalaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.sn3.21:2.2: Idha, mahārāja, ekacco puggalo nīce kule paccājāto hoti, caṇḍālakule vā venakule vā nesādakule vā rathakārakule vā pukkusakule vā dalidde appannapānabhojane kasiravuttike, yattha kasirena ghāsacchādo labbhati.
root_text.sn3.21:2.3: So ca hoti dubbaṇṇo duddasiko okoṭimako bavhābādho kāṇo vā kuṇī vā khañjo vā pakkhahato vā, na lābhī annassa pānassa vatthassa yānassa mālāgandhavilepanassa seyyāvasathapadīpeyyassa.
root_text.sn3.21:2.4: So kāyena duccaritaṁ carati, vācāya duccaritaṁ carati, manasā duccaritaṁ carati.
root_text.sn3.21:2.5: So kāyena duccaritaṁ caritvā vācāya duccaritaṁ caritvā manasā duccaritaṁ caritvā, kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.sn3.21:3.1: Seyyathāpi, mahārāja, puriso andhakārā vā andhakāraṁ gaccheyya, tamā vā tamaṁ gaccheyya, lohitamalā vā lohitamalaṁ gaccheyya.
root_text.sn3.21:3.3: Evaṁ kho, mahārāja, puggalo tamotamaparāyano hoti.
root_text.sn3.21:4.2: Idha, mahārāja, ekacco puggalo nīce kule paccājāto hoti, caṇḍālakule vā venakule vā nesādakule vā rathakārakule vā pukkusakule vā dalidde appannapānabhojane kasiravuttike, yattha kasirena ghāsacchādo labbhati.
root_text.sn3.21:4.3: So ca kho hoti dubbaṇṇo duddasiko okoṭimako bavhābādho, kāṇo vā kuṇī vā khañjo vā pakkhahato vā, na lābhī annassa pānassa vatthassa yānassa mālāgandhavilepanassa seyyāvasathapadīpeyyassa.
root_text.sn3.21:4.4: So kāyena sucaritaṁ carati, vācāya sucaritaṁ carati, manasā sucaritaṁ carati.
root_text.sn3.21:4.5: So kāyena sucaritaṁ caritvā vācāya sucaritaṁ caritvā manasā sucaritaṁ caritvā, kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.sn3.21:5.1: Seyyathāpi, mahārāja, puriso pathaviyā vā pallaṅkaṁ āroheyya, pallaṅkā vā assapiṭṭhiṁ āroheyya, assapiṭṭhiyā vā hatthikkhandhaṁ āroheyya, hatthikkhandhā vā pāsādaṁ āroheyya.
root_text.sn3.21:5.3: Evaṁ kho, mahārāja, puggalo tamojotiparāyano hoti.
root_text.sn3.21:6.2: Idha, mahārāja, ekacco puggalo ucce kule paccājāto hoti, khattiyamahāsālakule vā brāhmaṇamahāsālakule vā gahapatimahāsālakule vā, aḍḍhe mahaddhane mahābhoge pahūtajātarūparajate pahūtavittūpakaraṇe pahūtadhanadhaññe.
root_text.sn3.21:6.3: So ca hoti abhirūpo dassanīyo pāsādiko, paramāya vaṇṇapokkharatāya samannāgato, lābhī annassa pānassa vatthassa yānassa mālāgandhavilepanassa seyyāvasathapadīpeyyassa.
root_text.sn3.21:6.4: So kāyena duccaritaṁ carati, vācāya duccaritaṁ carati, manasā duccaritaṁ carati.
root_text.sn3.21:6.5: So kāyena duccaritaṁ caritvā vācāya duccaritaṁ caritvā manasā duccaritaṁ caritvā, kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.sn3.21:7.1: Seyyathāpi, mahārāja, puriso pāsādā vā hatthikkhandhaṁ oroheyya, hatthikkhandhā vā assapiṭṭhiṁ oroheyya, assapiṭṭhiyā vā pallaṅkaṁ oroheyya, pallaṅkā vā pathaviṁ oroheyya, pathaviyā vā andhakāraṁ paviseyya.
root_text.sn3.21:7.3: Evaṁ kho, mahārāja, puggalo jotitamaparāyano hoti.
root_text.sn3.21:8.2: Idha, mahārāja, ekacco puggalo ucce kule paccājāto hoti, khattiyamahāsālakule vā brāhmaṇamahāsālakule vā gahapatimahāsālakule vā, aḍḍhe mahaddhane mahābhoge pahūtajātarūparajate pahūtavittūpakaraṇe pahūtadhanadhaññe.
root_text.sn3.21:8.3: So ca hoti abhirūpo dassanīyo pāsādiko, paramāya vaṇṇapokkharatāya samannāgato, lābhī annassa pānassa vatthassa yānassa mālāgandhavilepanassa seyyāvasathapadīpeyyassa.
root_text.sn3.21:8.4: So kāyena sucaritaṁ carati, vācāya sucaritaṁ carati, manasā sucaritaṁ carati.
root_text.sn3.21:8.5: So kāyena sucaritaṁ caritvā vācāya sucaritaṁ caritvā manasā sucaritaṁ caritvā, kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.sn3.21:9.1: Seyyathāpi, mahārāja, puriso pallaṅkā vā pallaṅkaṁ saṅkameyya, assapiṭṭhiyā vā assapiṭṭhiṁ saṅkameyya, hatthikkhandhā vā hatthikkhandhaṁ saṅkameyya, pāsādā vā pāsādaṁ saṅkameyya.
root_text.sn3.21:9.3: Evaṁ kho, mahārāja, puggalo jotijotiparāyano hoti.
root_text.sn3.21:9.4: Ime kho, mahārāja, cattāro puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmin”ti.

translation_text.sn3.21:1.2: Then King Pasenadi of Kosala went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him:
translation_text.sn3.21:1.3: “Great king, these four individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.sn3.21:1.8: and the light bound for light.
translation_text.sn3.21:2.1: And how is an individual dark and bound for darkness?
translation_text.sn3.21:2.2: It’s when an individual is reborn in a low family—a family of corpse-workers, bamboo-workers, hunters, chariot-makers, or scavengers—poor, with little to eat or drink, where life is tough, and food and shelter are hard to find.
translation_text.sn3.21:2.3: And they’re ugly, unsightly, deformed, sickly—one-eyed, crippled, lame, or half-paralyzed. They don’t get to have food, drink, clothes, and vehicles; garlands, fragrance, and makeup; or bed, house, and lighting.
translation_text.sn3.21:2.4: And they do bad things by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.sn3.21:2.5: When their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell.
translation_text.sn3.21:3.1: This individual is like someone who goes from darkness to darkness, from blackness to blackness, from bloodstain to bloodstain.
translation_text.sn3.21:3.3: That’s how an individual is dark and bound for darkness.
translation_text.sn3.21:4.1: And how is an individual dark and bound for light?
translation_text.sn3.21:4.2: It’s when an individual is reborn in a low family—a family of corpse-workers, bamboo-workers, hunters, chariot-makers, or scavengers—poor, with little to eat or drink, where life is tough, and food and shelter are hard to find.
translation_text.sn3.21:4.3: And they’re ugly, unsightly, deformed, sickly—one-eyed, crippled, lame, or half-paralyzed. They don’t get to have food, drink, clothes, and vehicles; garlands, fragrance, and makeup; or bed, house, and lighting.
translation_text.sn3.21:4.4: But they do good things by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.sn3.21:4.5: When their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.sn3.21:5.1: This individual is like someone who ascends from the ground to a couch; from a couch to horseback; from horseback to an elephant; and from an elephant to a stilt longhouse.
translation_text.sn3.21:5.3: That’s how an individual is dark and bound for light.
translation_text.sn3.21:6.1: And how is an individual light and bound for darkness?
translation_text.sn3.21:6.2: It’s when an individual is reborn in an eminent family—a well-to-do family of aristocrats, brahmins, or householders—rich, affluent, and wealthy, with lots of gold and silver, lots of property and assets, and lots of money and grain.
translation_text.sn3.21:6.3: And they’re attractive, good-looking, lovely, of surpassing beauty. They get to have food, drink, clothes, and vehicles; garlands, fragrance, and makeup; and bed, house, and lighting.
translation_text.sn3.21:6.4: But they do bad things by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.sn3.21:6.5: When their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell.
translation_text.sn3.21:7.1: This individual is like someone who descends from a stilt longhouse to an elephant; from an elephant to horseback; from horseback to a couch; and from a couch to the ground; and from the ground they enter darkness.
translation_text.sn3.21:7.3: That’s how an individual is light and bound for darkness.
translation_text.sn3.21:8.1: And how is an individual light and bound for light?
translation_text.sn3.21:8.2: It’s when an individual is reborn in an eminent family—a well-to-do family of aristocrats, brahmins, or householders—rich, affluent, and wealthy, with lots of gold and silver, lots of property and assets, and lots of money and grain.
translation_text.sn3.21:8.3: And they’re attractive, good-looking, lovely, of surpassing beauty. They get to have food, drink, clothes, and vehicles; garlands, fragrance, and makeup; and bed, house, and lighting.
translation_text.sn3.21:8.4: And they do good things by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.sn3.21:8.5: When their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.sn3.21:9.1: This individual is like someone who shifts from one couch to another; from the back of one horse to another; from one elephant to another; or from one stilt longhouse to another.
translation_text.sn3.21:9.3: That’s how an individual is light and bound for light.
translation_text.sn3.21:9.4: These are the four individuals found in the world.”
translation_text.sn3.21:9.5: That is what the Buddha said. …
translation_text.sn3.21:10.4: they lack regard, they have wrong view.
translation_text.sn3.21:12.3: O king, ruler of the people:
translation_text.sn3.21:12.5: they fall into the terrible hell—
translation_text.sn3.21:12.6: from darkness they’re bound for darkness.
translation_text.sn3.21:13.1: O king, some people are poor,
translation_text.sn3.21:13.3: They give with best of intentions,
translation_text.sn3.21:14.1: They rise for and bow to
translation_text.sn3.21:15.1: they don’t prevent others from giving
translation_text.sn3.21:15.3: O king, ruler of the people:
translation_text.sn3.21:15.5: they go to the Third Heaven—
translation_text.sn3.21:15.6: from darkness they’re bound for light.
translation_text.sn3.21:16.1: O king, some people are rich,
translation_text.sn3.21:16.4: they lack regard, they have wrong view.
translation_text.sn3.21:18.3: O king, ruler of the people:
translation_text.sn3.21:18.5: they fall into the terrible hell—
translation_text.sn3.21:18.6: from light they’re bound for darkness.
translation_text.sn3.21:19.1: O king, some people are rich,
translation_text.sn3.21:19.3: They give with best of intentions,
translation_text.sn3.21:20.1: They rise for and bow to
translation_text.sn3.21:21.1: they don’t prevent others from giving
translation_text.sn3.21:21.3: O king, ruler of the people:
translation_text.sn3.21:21.5: they go to the Third Heaven—
translation_text.sn3.21:21.6: from light they’re bound for light.”
